rows: Avalon Hotel and Bungalows Palm Springs

Staff were wonderful and the grounds are very nice, but our room was horrible. It used to be a great place to stay, but there is zero decor on the walls, our bathroom door wouldn’t even close, and the couch in our room reminded us of something from IKEA. For over $600 a night, we expected to at least have some type of view, but nothing and the windows had film on them so you couldn’t see out. It’s like being in a motel!
I would not reccomend this hotel!

The hotel overall is shabby and careworn. There are paint scrapes all over the place, and badly scuffed, slummy looking floors in the guest corridors.In our room, the bathroom, in addtion to substandard lighting and the presence of a badly banged up table whose function remains a mystery, had stone tile long overdue for regrouting.The ceiling track for the curtain in the room had been pulled out of the ceiling and the curtain itself was clearly too long for the windo opening and stained with rust.The sliding door to the patio had been damaged as well.The lighitng in the room was poor, cheap and substandard.  The room overall had a dated and shabby appearance.The hotel also slapped a $50 per day resort fee onto our bill which was not mentioned in the expedia listing. They also dedcusted $200 for "incidentals", which we had no control over.In our case both of these were refunded when we complained about the condtion of the property.

This hotel is not worth the expense. I would also say that it’s deceptive to charge a $57 fee for using the “resort.” As far as I could tell there was only a small pool, which is equivalent to what you’d get at a Holiday Inn. There were no tennis courts, golf course, spa or anything else that I would typically associate with a resort. So why are customers being charged a resort usage fee!? I also don’t believe that I saw that  indicated on the website. But that wasn’t my biggest issue with staying there. In general, I felt the rooms were rundown, and the hot water was turned off from 4 to 7 PM which basically meant that we couldn’t shower after hiking all day and had to cancel our dinner plans. If I return to Palm Springs, I won’t be staying there.

Been staying at this property for nearly 30 years, also been a vendor for Marriot for over a decade so well aware of the concessions that can be made to accommodate guests, and none were taken during our week reservation on Christmas week. We were kicked out and had our reservation cancelled for our Christmas vacation because this pet friendly hotel allows dogs, but not our five month old, less than 5 lbs baby kitten.Management stated that our kitten was more of a risk than a loud, potentially damaging dog. Tried to also tell us it was an allergen issue, yet people with dog allergies are not considered?They could have easily done some investigating and realized that not only is our breed one of the most hypoallergenic of all cats, but at 5 lbs she is also silent, and still has her baby teeth and baby coat, and is basically harmless in all ways possible. Instead a blanket policy of “Yes Dogs, No Cats,” was applied at this failing hotel. It’s worth noting that we had already checked in, and were headed to the pool with no problem from the front desk who petted and held our kitten during the check in process before a manager then kicked us out.Other messes at the Avalon:1) no more valet. Guests are now left to fend for themselves.2) no welcome beverage upon arrival.3) messy and dirty lobby with stacks of boxes behind the check in window.4) unprofessional looking staff with no uniforms.5) zero holiday decor.Everything happens for a reason, and we ended up getting a gorgeous large bungalow with a private patio at The Colony Palms for our tiny family member. Valet carried our bags, we were greeted with a glass of champagne, and there is a gorgeous Christmas tree in the lobby with professionally dressed staff. Literally a perfect welcome for our whole family.Needless to say, the Avalon has lost its magic and after over 30 stays at the property since it was the Viceroy in the 1990’s… we will never be back. This situation could have absolutely been avoided in the name of customer service.If you’ve stayed here before, you’ll be shocked like we were to see it looking so poorly, run down, and sub-par. If you’re an animal lover, you’d also love to hear how this now run down property treated two loyal guests on Christmas. What a mess.We are thrilled to be at The Colony Palms instead. We suggest you take our advice and do the same.

Total mixed bag and would likely not stay here again.  Front desk staff is friendly and gave us a warm greeting upon arrival and was very helpful with the variety of challenges we had during our stay.Pros: front desk staff, easy walking distance to hiking and all that downtown PS has to offer, our terrace had a nice view of the mountains, well decorated pool area.Cons: no coffee maker in the room (very odd for a supposed 4 star hotel); the worst housekeeping I've ever experienced - we'd ask for towels and they'd bring bath mats, they didn't empty the trash, they didn't clean up obvious stains and mess, we ended up putting the trash in the hallway just to get it out of the room and then 3 other people on the floor did the same; room is also cheaply decorated and oddly missing furniture - it was big, but there was no chair and in the bathroom it was also well sized, but only had a tiny table to put your toiletries on - very odd!, there actually is free reserved parking behind the hotel but it took a few days before we were able to get an answer on that; the free coffee and pastries in the AM quickly ran out of pastriesOverall, it just wasn't a great experience, but you can see the potential if someone actually cared

Booked a 4 night stay through Chase's "The Edit" Collection.  Spent one night and promptly checked out the next morning.  Basically was willing to walk away and lose my entire booking cost...it was that bad.   This is basically an old motel that was repainted white (including the tiled floors) and called "Boutique".  Walls were paper thin.  Could hear peoples conversation in room above late into the night.  Baby crying at 5am sounded like it was in our unit.  People walking above sounded like stomping or banging on our ceiling.  Just a total lack of insulation which was not addressed whenever they remodeled this motel. Bathroom was tiny and dingy.  Shower had mold growing all over.Checked out after one terrible night and asked for bill. still haven't gotten it one week later, but they added charges I never incurred to my entirely prepaid stay that I abandoned. Can't wait to fight this.   This should not be on Chase's "The Edit" collection.  I would look elsewhere if you're looking for quiet and an elevated experience.
The room feels like a dated 60s home when through a bad renovation job. It’s nothing like their pictures. We also saw a lot of ants crawling around the room through out our 3-day stay.

First, I want to share that the staff was incredible.  My review has nothing to do with the staff. That said, this is not at all what the pictures reflect on their website.  I was so disappointed when we arrived at this hotel.  The room was dirty, it smelled and it was very old. They put newer furniture in there to try and modernized it, but they just put lipstick on a pig...at the end of the day it was still a pig. The walls and ceilings were very thin...we heard the people upstairs from us as if they were in the room with us, the staff walked outside pushing laundry and garbage carts at all hours...we did not once get a good night sleep the entire 3 days. I kept finding bugs walking across the floor...it was just gross. The parking is horrendous and the hotel itself is not clearly marked so finding it at night proved to be quite difficult.  They fitness center lacked luster and again isn't was is presented on the website.  Please beware before booking here...and if you do, please don't have high expectations based on what you see online...if you don't expect too much, you'll be fine.

This would have been a five star until my last hour on the property.  I’ve stay so many times in the past when it was the Vicroy and Avalon.  I was relaxing at the pool and was asked to leave the property by 4PM.  Honestly, very disturbed by this as I was truly enjoying quiet relaxing my time.  Not even sure what to say.  Really deeply disturbed is the only way I will be feeling when I return home.
It was a nice property, but everyone in our group got bits from bugs in the room.

All week long I've been trying to rehearse my review for this property. I am torn and have more negatives than positives. Based upon their website and the bonvoy app, I had a certain expectation for this property. I booked a studio kings, based on the description, photos and the desire for a patio / balcony. I adored the decor and mid century feel of the room. After a very complicated and long day of travel, we arrived to check in. We were given room 511 and told that no maps were available, and that our room was near the on site parking lot ( we didn't require the use of a bellman due to misrouted luggage). It took us about ten minutes to finally find the room.  It was around the corner from the property laundry, down a narrow walkway  . The room was not what I had booked or expected. I was expecting mid century glamor. The room was kndded, a one bedroom, but it looked like a college kids apartment. Stark white, void of any character, decor or charm. The properties laundry was on the other side of the room, and the lights shook when they used the laundry machines. This was not the experience that I was expecting.  This type of room should be given to someone who books thru a third party website, not a bonvoy member, booking thru their app.  I have no idea why we were given such a "ghetto" room, as the property was far from being high occupancy .  We did utilize the Presidio pool, which was lovely, but service was non existent. We ended up ordering food and drinks at Chi Chi. The chi chi pool was closed as they were pressure washing the area.  Sadly they sent me a survey four days ago, and they have yet to respond to any of my comments.

"James at front desk was very accommodating when I called for a last minute reservation. Upon checking in, we were given a very warm welcome, great detailed information about hotel and also area restaurants. Anything I asked for or needed during stay was taken care of almost immediately!"

I want to thank the staff at the Avalon hotel  for the warmth welcome and the excellent service, we loved our stay. Room was very comfortable and welcoming. The atmosphere all around was very peaceful.  Juan was able to acomodate us to a very nice couples massage.Eloy was very helpful giving us food recommendations by the pool. Definitely a 10/10 experience.  We will be back soon.

We got married at the Avalon on 10/25/25 and had the most incredible experience - words cannot express how special it was! We booked our venue almost 2 years before our wedding day so we knew very early on in our planning that this was where we wanted to have our big day but we couldn't have even imagined how incredible the actual day would end up being. The venue itself is SO special and creates such an amazing guest experience. Because each part of the event takes place in a different space with a different energy / backdrop (ceremony in the courtyard, cocktail hour by the pool, dinner outside, party in the ballroom) it creates so much movement in a really seamless way and led guests to feel excited for what was to come next. We loved that component of the venue and it was a selling point for us but we didn't realize just how much our guests would appreciate it. It kept the event feeling fresh (vs. being in one stale location for hours) and kept the energy levels high amongst everyone. This was such an underrated bonus!The food was absolutely incredible and the chef and bartender were so willing to work with us to personalize our menu and make it unique to us. We wanted to weave some of our story into our meal and drinks and they took our feedback and implemented it so well. So many of our guests said this was the best wedding food they have ever had. This was a real surprise to us as a lot of times plated meals at weddings are not the best, so we didn't know what to expect (even after doing the tasting).Felicity, our main point of contact on the Avalon team, was so great to work with. She coordinated seamlessly with our wedding planner and assisted with any requests we had. We were in such good hands with her and knew everything would go off without a hitch on the day of thanks to her partnership with our planner! Special shoutout to Abbie from the Avalon team who helped coordinate our room block and was also such a pleasure to work with!All in all, it was the most perfect day at the most perfect place. As with any wedding, there are small things that go wrong and things you would do differently but choosing the Avalon is something we would do over and over again. Many people said this was the best wedding they've ever been to, and yes we are biased, but we think so too and so much of that is thanks to having our big day at this one-in-a-million place! THANK YOU FOR EVERYTHING!!!

I had a great experience at the Avalon. I found it and booked it through the Capital One travel portal, where the hotel was included in their Premiere Collection. I found the property to be beautiful, with really well kept landscaping and varied pool choices. That said, the buildings do show a little bit their age on the outside. The room I had (Regency) was really nice, very clean and had everything I needed. Breakfast on both days was wonderful, I had an avocado toast as well as the Avalon breakfast - both delicious and filling. The staff I encountered were all very nice and helpful, particularly one of the guys at the reception (I forgot his name but he was great!). The only downside to my stay was that the guests in the room above us were super loud and constantly walked with shoes/heels on, even past midnight. Of course, this is not the hotels fault, but maybe it would be a good idea if the hotel provided white noise machines or something of that sort. Overall a great experience, particularly with the Capital One experience where you are provided dining credits.

We absolutely love this property! Great location and great Palm Springs vibe!  The people here are so welcoming and helpful. Special shout out to Jon who took amazing care of us in the bar/restsurant. We left feeling like family. We will definitely be back!

A great time! We stayed there over Christmas and the entire team from check-in, to housekeeping to the bar, restaurant team was amazing. It really impressed me that this property delivered such a great experience over Christmas.

Avalon delivers a modern twist on the classic Hollywood of Palm Springs. The hotel provides excellent customer service in a convenient, walkable setting. We enjoyed the pool areas and high-quality breakfasts at Chi Chi. The room itself was large and comfortable. Insider's tip: While you're staying here, check out the amenities and unique history at the sister location, Inglewood, next door. Melvin's is a must for dinner.

This hotel is not worth the expense. I would also say that it’s deceptive to charge a $57 fee for using the “resort.” As far as I could tell there was only a small pool, which is equivalent to what you’d get at a Holiday Inn. There were no tennis courts, golf course, spa or anything else that I would typically associate with a resort. So why are customers being charged a resort usage fee!? I also don’t believe that I saw that  indicated on the website. But that wasn’t my biggest issue with staying there. In general, I felt the rooms were rundown, and the hot water was turned off from 4 to 7 PM which basically meant that we couldn’t shower after hiking all day and had to cancel our dinner plans. If I return to Palm Springs, I won’t be staying there.

Really enjoyed our 3 night stay at the Avalon.  We were a family of 5 staying in a 2-bedroom bungalow. Very comfortable and spacious.  The staff was friendly, the pools and grounds were clean and well maintained.  Enjoyed breakfast in the restaurant and happy hour. Location couldn't be beat - walking distance to the main street!

Good: Beautiful grounds meticulously landscaped in an excellent location.  Peaceful quiet setting was super low key.Bad: Main restaurant dinner menu could have some more options but the food was very good and breakfast was awesome!

Great location. Walking distance to bars, restaurants, hiking, and museums. Meticulously manicured lawns and public spaces. The absolute best service. From check-in to check-out (and even some hours after due to a late flight), the staff truly made us feel welcome and went out of their way for us. The breakfast at the Chi Chi lounge is a must. The rooms are rad. Stellar vibe. 12/10

Wow, we loved this hotel. The staff are friendly and knowledgeable, and the clientele are very respectful. The food was good and I especially appreciated the value of the happy hour. The amenities are well maintained and you should stay here for the pool alone. Excellent location. I loved the spa. If you go to the spa, please ask for Randi. She’s incredible.If I had one negative comment, it would be that I found the bed a bit uncomfortable. I remedied this easily though with extra blankets, which functioned as a mattress pad. My partner had no issues though and I think it’s a matter of personal taste!

Okay so this place is lovely. We had a suite which was bigger than our apartment. The grounds are beautiful. It’s right in the middle of Palm Springs near any restaurant you want or the art gallery or shops. Free coffee and pastries every morning. Very happy to see dogs.That all said, there was some weirdness that really detracted from the stay which, considering the price, I found difficult to ignore. The suite comes with a kitchen with a stove and fridge and full counters and everything, however there’s barely any cooking equipment and what there is is old and tired. You have a resort fee so you can access the 3 pools plus spa which means the sauna, steam room, cold plunge and jacuzzi. One day there was a wedding so they kick everyone out all of the pools at 3pm for the rest of the day. The next day the spa tells everyone they can’t access their facilities anymore and the manager puts out reserved signs everywhere to gate keep the jacuzzi area because they have people having services (they had a couple of people) even though the website says priority for loungers is for hotel guests?! She was rude and strange and basically tried to prevent guests using what we had paid for without reason. After complaining I was refunded just one day resort fees even though the spa situation impacted two days and the pool situation our first day.It’s just not something you expect when you’re paying a lot for a hotel as a treat on a special occasion. To be treated like nuisances is not what you expect.

Good: Beautiful grounds meticulously landscaped in an excellent location.  Peaceful quiet setting was super low key.Bad: Main restaurant dinner menu could have some more options but the food was very good and breakfast was awesome!

Stayed for a business conference and couldn’t have been more satisfied with the property and overwhelmed by the service in their lobby bar.John/ Jon behind the bar helped keep my evenings intriguing with his excellent service and over-the-top knowledge when it came to helping me choose which spirit(s) to enjoy each night. I’ll give him or whoever orders for a bar a pass on the DAOU selection - I just feel it’s an underperforming wine with so many better options from Paso available - The Fableist being one of my highest recommendations.Anyways - thank you Avalon, thank you John/ Jon for turning a boring business conference into a relaxing, entertaining and insightful stay in December!

I work professionally in Historic Building Preservation and have wanted to stay at the Avalon and Ingleside for years and finally got the chance. These historic hotels are right in the heart of old school Palm Springs. The grounds and Mountain views are ridiculously gorgeous. You will have four amazing pools and two hot tubs to choose from if you count the use of their sister property The Ingleside near by. I got a massage at their Estrella Spa. Randi gives a world class massage and is just delightful. Avalon’s poolside Chi Chi Restaurant has a small but perfect menu from breakfast, fresh salads, and burgers.For the ultimate authentic Rat Pack dinner, Melvyn’s on the grounds of the Ingleside is everything! Finally, I want to also call out James at the front desk for making my stay extra perfect. Staying here is a dream. I’d live on site if I could 😭

The family and I had a wonderful stay at the Avalon hotel in Palm Springs. We stayed in a one bedroom bungalow near the family pool area. The rooms were clean, comfortable, and the kitchenette was perfect for storing snacks and drinks. The hotel staff was super friendly, from the front desk to the wait staff at the Chi Chi restaurant. They even let our kids feed the koi fish, which they loved. We traveled in early December and it never felt busy or crowded. Family pool was very nice and relaxing and the hot tub area was also wonderful. Breakfast at the Chi Chi was also great. Food was excellent and they had a lot of options for the kids and parents. We stayed in the secured parking area that was super convenient. Street parking was also an option but did notice that was limited during various times of day. Would recommend this place for families and hope to go back again in the future!

Good: , The location was excellent and both pools were very warm.Bad: Didn't seem tidy and there was no attention to detail.  When we arrived, they gave me a piece of paper with some rules to sign that looked like interrogation.  Aweful, after being in traffic for 4 hours.  Some landscaping look untidy and there is dust on evergreen hedges.  The piano player at Ingleside's Melvyn's was not very friendly.   On check-out, they proceeded to automatically add Amenities Fee, but they didn't tell us that this includes tea and scones, or that it includes the pool at Ingleside. The shower was not working properly and  curtain didn't slide well. There was no kettle or tea in our room.  The staff at the Avalon bar next to reception was very casual, just flirting between each other and ignoring us.  We didn't think this stay was as special or nice as the one with Viceroy.  We remembered the amazing Viceroy Hotel, how glamorous and gorgeous the adult pool was.  This hotel is nothing like it.  Disappointed that it had to change.  THe wedding venues were not well-utilised, that area seems wasted and empty.

Everything was great. Beautiful, well maintained hotel. Nice staff and great amenities. The pools were nice. The fitness facility was great and the Pelotons were a nice addition. The food was also excellent. The room was nice. No issues at all.
Jon at the bar has always provided the best experience.  I continually go back to see him.  Good selection of food and drinks.

Avalon is part of an older historic property along with Inglewood Estate. The hotel shows its age, and for someone that prefers newer updated hotels, I actually really enjoyed my stay here and loved the experience and charm of the hotel grounds, restaurants, and wonderful hospitality. Our room was large and spacious and very comfortable. Enjoyed breakfast by the pool daily and slept like a baby. The staff were incredibly attentive and helpful. It’s also in a great location away from the downtown noise but close enough to walk to it. All around a very enjoyable stay.

The front desk staff was friendly and welcoming. I ordered dinner at the bar where Jon, the bartender, was able to recommend drinks and anticipate what I wanted. Jon is fun and friendly while handling multiple orders and interacting with all of us. Good food, great drinks and a friendly welcoming atmosphere…highly recommend!
The exteriors are beautiful as ever, but some of the room & restaurant decor that Kelly Werstler designed has been altered & downgraded, not updated for better.

Loved our stay at the Avalon and spring break in Palm Springs. The location was fabulous, and the property was very clean. The staff were amazing and so pleasant. We especially enjoyed the bar. The vibes were so chill and the bartender, Jon, took such great care of us!

We had a trip down to Palm Springs and the coast in mid November. We booked the Avalon Hotel which is a short walk to the main strip.We checked in by Aris and Lynn. Both were excellent. We were not 100% happy with our first room, so they moved us to a beautiful bungalow. We stayed a total of five nights and thoroughly enjoyed our stay. Very boutique like. Nice pools and the  Chi Chi restaurant was great. The staff there were totally friendly as well and food was great. They had coffee and danish in the am at 7 am as complimentary.We enjoyed the hotel very much and had lots of laughs with the front desk-bellman as well as Candice.Looking forward to going back!

This property is absolutely stunning and such a great “Palm Springs” vibe. Whenever I visit Palm Springs, I always make sure to hit up the hotel bar, even if I don’t stay at the Avalon. Jon is an excellent bartender and always makes us feel like old friends. As a sober person, it can be alienating and uncomfortable to hang out in bars when everyone is trying new drinks and having a great time. Jon always makes me feel included by making awesome “mocktails” that mimic what others are drinking and always has new alcohol free drink ideas for me to try. It really makes all the difference. Can’t wait to go back!

My husband and I first came to the Avalon when it was still the Viceroy for my husband's 30th birthday. He recently turned 45 and we have been coming for special occasions, holidays, and getaways multiple times a year since. In the fifteen years that we have been coming we have traveled extensively throughout the world, but there is nothing quite like returning "home" to the Avalon. There have been times throughout the years when just walking through that front gate, seeing the koi, and that big beautiful door transported us into our happy place. But over these past few years, probably since covid, it has been walking through the lobby and then into the beautiful bar that has done the trick. Jonathan Saindon is my favorite bartender in the world, and this strip of mirrors and sparkling lights that is the bar at Avalon is where he works his magic. Jonathan has been here for every single stay, including the weird covid times, when we had our two week old daughter with us, my son's 3rd birthday, my staff retreats, girlfriend parties, couples getaways, and on and on. He is professional, warm, funny, lighthearted, and most importantly a really good person. He makes awesome drinks, but he does more than that - he gives this place a soul, even when it has gone through transitions in the past. He has been the constant for us, and we look forward to seeing him each time.We love the three pools at the Avalon, particularly the one framed by the iconic wispy palms. The rooms are charming and the grounds are as well - it's like stepping into a historic oasis from another time. We will no doubt continue spending significant milestones at the Avalon and are grateful for it as a mainstay throughout the years.

Visited most recently with family. It’s a wonderful property with amazing staff and close to everything downtown. We have always felt valued when visiting the Avalon Palm Springs. The bar “Chi Chi” is one of our favourite places to relax and hang  of an evening, and a huge shout out to Jon . Jon always has a big smile and remembers us from our previous visits - makes us feel like part of the Avalon family. Thank you!

Had a wonderful weekend at the Avalon hotel.Welcomed with nice front deck staff.Our room overlooked the pool and we were steps away from the BAR! We had dinner and drinks with a bartender Jhon who really knew his drinks and gave us excellent service. We will be back next month.
This place is amazing, the outside dining area is beautiful, I like siting at the bar Jon is great, engaging and very friendly, we had the Cobb and a wonderful martini! We will be back
Items were missing on an order and the prices were unnecessarily high
The menu is limited, and truly the food options and the food left a lot to be desired.
The waffle fries were not crispy or flavorful. The flavor powder on them didn't really add anything and may have taken away. Was the ketchup something other than Heinz?
Disappointed there was no readily available Decaf coffee
More lunch options would have been great
Coffee drinks were pricey but appreciated free coffee service!
I ordered a side of scrambled eggs. The eggs seemed to be made from powdered eggs. Not good!!! Everything else was delicious!
We had breakfast twice, very good, morning coffee was delicious. Perhaps a bigger service table as it stayed busy.
The breakfast menu and food was very good. The dessert menu - cookies and ice cream sandwich was disgusting. The ice cream sandwich was so frozen it was unedible. The cookies were from Vons.
Chips and guacamole were good. Salsa could be more flavorful. Great poolside service.
I just feel the hotel should have another option for food.  Chi Chi was fine for casual dining but not great for dinner.
Only  stopped in for a pre-dinner drink.  Nice experience.
Food took a little long most times but was great. Would have wanted breakfast past 11 on a Saturday but your menu changed to lunch. That’s too early on a weekend.
Food was great. Service v good and prompt. Choices seemed a bit limited.
confused over gratuity, believe it was 20% applied to room service. If this is the case, don't ask for additional gratuity on top of the 20%. I don't like that.
We only did kids menu which was a bit limited.
tray was not picked up at night.  It was sitting in front of the door which I almost stepped into when leaving the room in the morning.
I ordered in room dining and the steak frites was excellent, very good steak.

Room service breakfast was great but my coffee and latte were served in "to-go" cups....for my room.   I had expected a cup saucier, coffee service experience for my patio/room. If I wanted "to-go" I would have gone to the lobby coffee service on my way out.
My only wish is that we had more to choose from, everything we tried was great but it would be great if there were maybe specials of the day or maybe a little more variety.
Guest had issues with the AC not going lower than 70 degrees as well as saying the bed was just a box spring with a mattres pad.

Guest in 521 reported warm AC at 3:56 PM and requested Engineering after she left the room. I offered immediate assistance, but she preferred to wait until after her shower. She later called the Front Desk, and we dispatched Engineering and offered fans. Ms. Vance called back upset about the delay. Engineering confirmed the AC unit was down. I apologized and offered a new suite with working AC and full assistance with moving. She became frustrated about the inconvenience and missing her event. I offered for her to attend the event first and move afterward. She continued to raise her voice and mentioned a medical condition. I asked her to stop yelling as I was trying to assist her and provide her an imedate solutaion and offered medical assistance as well, which she said she would request if needed.
Front Office - Not satisfied with room

After checking in guest went into the room and stated they hated the size of the room. Guest was then shown an available room, guest wanted it but it was sold for a last minute booking, guest was shown another room.
Guest was adamant she requested a quiet room. However, upon arrival to the room guest stated they wanted a quieter room because they heard music from the pool in their room.

Guest came to the front desk stating she will not be moving rooms because they already moved rooms three times during their stay. Once from Ingleside, a second time from the one bedroom stuite, a second time to a 2 bendroom bungalow, and now to the 1 bedroom bungalow.
Apologized and offered to move the luggage myself from the room while alone at the front desk.

Guest was scheduled to move to a 1bng today from their 2bngdue to availability. Guest had been made fully aware of this by a manager earlier in the week. Once being told they needed to move today they became very upset and stated they had been told  by a manager that they no longer needed to move and were upset they were now being told they needed to move.
Guest states from arrival to the room was not a good vibe and nothing like what he read about.
Refunded 6 days and waived resort fee.
Guest departed 3/17 and called this morning very upset stating we charged her twice.

Guest checked into 521, they later called stating that the room was at 90 degrees and if we could send some fans over. Offered to move them room but it would only be available for the 1st two nights of their stay and they would have to move room the last night. They agreed and moved them to out 2bedroom bungalow as a comp for the inconvenience.
Comp upgrade to 2BNG and 1 BNG for the last night
Guest called the front desk stating their fire alarm was making a beeping sound so the batteries needed to be replaced. They casually added there were cockroaches in the room.
The guest reached out to inform us that the rates charged for the nights of the 10th and 11th do not match the rates they were originally quoted at the time of booking.
Guest wanted to depart a day early simply because they wanted to go home.
Front Office - Not satisfied with room
guest had a rough night's sleep because she felt like she was sleeping on the box spring.
Removed the night of resort fees. $57.34
Guest says AC in room is not cool enough at 70 degrees
Oven and stove not working 2 days in a row and they did a big grocery shopping so they could cook. They chose not to have engineering in their room again today.
$150.00 F&B and removed 2 nights resort fees.

The guest has departed, and after their departure, Front Desk was informed by the occupant of Room 323 that an incorrect room number had been provided for their Chi Chi dinner charge totaling $1,185.13.
$1,185.13 adjusted off their folio and posted to correct room.
Guest expressed that they were disapointed that their 2BNG did not have a stove like the previous visits.
Removed Resort Fee for one night and applied a $150 F&B credit
